academics.rigor_self_rating: Pretty intense — 3 APs and high-level math
academics.above_and_beyond: Won the school hackathon last year. Did an independent study on machine learning my sophomore year because the school didn't offer it. Got published in a student research journal for my project on detecting fake news with ML.
academics.approach_to_school: I'd rather be building something. School works when teachers let me apply concepts. It doesn't when it's just memorization.

things_built: Built 3 iOS apps — one is a homework planner with 400 active users, one is a fake-news detector (my research project), one is a personal habit tracker for me. All on GitHub.
Co-founded a small AI tutoring side business with my friend — we charge $30/hour to tutor middle schoolers in math using AI-assisted study plans we generate. We've made about $2,800 since September.
Run a Discord server for high school coders — 320 members, weekly challenges I write.
Built a custom mechanical keyboard with hand-soldered switches (just for fun, but I'm proud of it)
Wrote a Python script my dad uses for his accounting work — saves him about 6 hours a week
clubs_leadership: Computer Science Club — President, founded the club my sophomore year
work: Summer internship at a local accounting firm doing data entry + Python automation (informal, through family connection)
activities.community_service: Teach a free Saturday morning coding class at the Stamford Public Library for kids 8-12. Started it sophomore year, still running it.

identity.first_gen_college: No — both parents have bachelor's degrees, my mom has an MBA
identity.other_background: Both parents are engineers. Lots of expectation to go into CS or engineering.

financial_reality.family_situation: We can pay full price for state schools but need help for private schools. Looking for merit aid.
financial_reality.fafsa_eligible: Yes, but probably not much need-based aid
financial_reality.working_during_college: I'll probably do internships during summers and contract work during school

what_i_want.dream_vibe: Somewhere serious about CS but not just CS people. I don't want to be in a coding cave for 4 years.
what_i_want.city_vs_campus: Either works, slight preference for college town with a real campus feel
what_i_want.distance_from_home: Different region — I want to leave the East Coast bubble
what_i_want.size_preference: Medium (2K-10K) — big enough for research opportunities, small enough for professors to know me
what_i_want.diversity: Somewhat — I want intellectual diversity more than demographic
what_i_want.weather: Mild winter preferred but I can handle real winter
what_i_want.instant_no: Cutthroat CS program where everyone's just trying to beat each other
what_i_want.instant_yes: Strong CS program + active maker culture (hackathons, project showcases) + chance to do real research

career_direction.today_pick: Software engineer, possibly with a side path into entrepreneurship or product management
career_direction.miserable_in_job: Maintaining legacy code at a giant corporation. No autonomy. No interesting problems.

real_talk.schools_should_know: Look at my GitHub. The classroom is not where I prove myself. My apps have real users. My business has real revenue. The grades-only schools probably aren't right for me.
real_talk.worry: Going to a school that's all theory and no building. Also debt — my parents won't help much if I go private and don't get merit aid.
real_talk.success_in_4_years: Graduating from a school where I've shipped real projects, with internship experience at companies I respect, on track to start my own thing or join a startup as an early engineer.
real_talk.anything_else: Don't just give me MIT/Stanford/CMU. I know those are reaches. Show me schools with strong CS that I have a real shot at AND that fit how I actually work.
